title: New Lead — AI Voice Clone for Real Estate Wholesaler (Demo in 24hrs)
content:
# AI Voice Clone System — Real Estate Lead
*April 14, 2026*

---

## What They Want
An AI system that clones their voice so their appointment setters can type a message and it gets delivered as a personalized voice message that sounds exactly like them.

**Core use case:**
- Instagram DMs: appointment setter types a response, AI converts it to the lead's actual voice
- Voicemails: automated but sounds like the real person
- Tailored to each seller's specific situation (real estate sellers have personal problems, financial issues, etc.)
- People lower their guard when they hear a real voice vs reading a text

## Why This Matters to Them
- Real estate wholesaling is deeply relational. People want to talk to the actual person, not a bot.
- They don't want to do sales calls forever. This is their exit strategy from being the bottleneck.
- Voice messages are their biggest conversion tool right now. They want to scale that without losing the personal touch.
- They want to build with the intention of eventually exiting the day-to-day sales role.

## What They DON'T Want
- Mass automation (like Mochi/ManyChat style). They specifically said NOT general scope.
- They want it focused and strategic: just the voice piece, tailored to them.
- Not trying to automate deal underwriting or relational judgment calls. AI can't know a seller is difficult or that someone will randomly hit you up on Friday at 6pm. That stays human.

## Competition Reference
- They're in a program with someone named Nick who built software called "Mochi"
- Mochi is broader Instagram DM automation
- They want something more focused: specifically the voice cloning piece, not the mass automation

## Connection
- Know each other from Eddie Cumberbatch's course/community
- Have talked before in the past, reconnecting now
- They've been watching Ryan's content on TikTok
- Referenced School Games winners, Pierre, Shari (tech sales), Hormozi

## Ryan's Commitment
- Demo in 24 hours
- Send demo, get feedback, iterate
- Schedule a call after the demo to discuss next steps
- If it fits, move forward. If not, adjust.

## Technical Approach
- Voice cloning API (ElevenLabs or similar) trained on their voice samples
- Simple interface: appointment setter types message → AI generates voice clip in their voice
- Deliver via Instagram DM voice note or as a voicemail
- Could integrate with their existing CRM/workflow

## Next Steps
1. Build voice clone demo (24hr deadline)
2. Send demo for review
3. Schedule follow-up call
4. Iterate based on feedback

title: Meeting Notes: Coach Steve Call #2 - April 13, 2026
content:
# Coach Steve Discovery Call #2 - April 13, 2026

**Format:** Phone/Video call

## Key Findings

### How Clubs Find Players Today
- **Word of mouth only.** No portal. No centralized system.
- Parents/players email clubs directly
- Clubs bring players in for trial practices, then evaluate
- Steve confirmed: "There's no portal for clubs"

### What Coaches Actually Look At on a Player
- What club the player currently plays for (this signals their level)
- Height, weight, physical measurables
- Whether they play high school ball
- Additional experience/background
- **Level determines where they trial:** "DYSC Rec" player goes to EDP tryout. "Hartford Athletic" player goes straight to ECNL.
- Example from Steve: "If I see somebody that says DYSC Rec, I'm not necessarily going to bring them to an ECNL practice, but maybe I'll bring them to an EDP one. But if someone says they're playing for Hartford Athletic, then it's like, okay, they come with a good resume."

### Target Market (Shifted from Original)
- **Primary:** Higher-level players - ECNL, MLS Next, USL Academy, USL2
  - Players trying to get into NYCFC, Red Bulls level programs
  - Pre-professional players are the MOST desperate for opportunities
  - Steve: "This has to be the higher level players. If those guys can have access to all these players, I feel like it would be a home run."
- **Secondary:** Relocation market
  - Families moving between states need club discovery
  - Summer is peak relocation season (kids out of school)
- **Big Insight:** USL Academy and USL2 have NO transfer portal at all. These are players pursuing semi-pro and professional careers with zero centralized discovery tool.

### Steve's Network
- Can call **20+ club directors** and they would answer the phone or respond to email
- Has hundreds/thousands of player contacts for beta testing
- "I don't see that being an issue" on getting users
- Willing to reach out to clubs nationwide, not just CT

### Steve's Role
- Connecting with clubs in CT and across the country
- Conducting discovery interviews
- **Not comfortable running social media** - "I'm not very good at running social media"
- Has a marketing guy who could join for equity, but only wants to bring him in once there's a deck/prototype
- Day to day: reaching out to clubs, talking to parents, leveraging relationships

### Club Concern
- Still worried about AC Connecticut seeing the platform negatively
- Steve's framing solution: position it initially as a **relocation tool**, not a transfer market
- "How we put it into the industry and how we market it for now needs to be kind of like a relocation type of thing"
- Then it naturally grows into the full transfer portal

### Naming Issue
- Can't use "transfer market" - US doesn't have transfer fees like Europe
- Need a different name: "portal" or "platform" framing
- Steve asked Brayan to use AI to generate name ideas
- "One Ball One Dream" is the brand, product needs its own name

### Timeline
- Beta: August 2026 (could be earlier depending on discovery speed)
- Full launch: End of 2026
- Steve's note: August might miss peak relocation season (summer), but okay to soft launch and go full for summer 2027
- Discovery data collection target: next week

## Platform Features Confirmed by Steve
- Filter by position, age, skill level, travel distance (10/20/30 min radius)
- Map view showing clubs by level (MLS, ECNL, EDP, travel, rec)
- One-click communication with coaches/clubs
- Player profiles with highlights, stats, current club, measurables
- Club-side search: "We need a goalkeeper in a 20 mile radius" and filter
- Dual roster capability (travel + premier/USL)
- AI-powered league acronym recognition (ECNL, ECRL, EDP, USL, NPSL, UPSL)

## Next Steps
1. **Brayan:** Send Steve interview questions for parents, directors, and players
2. **Brayan:** Send AI note-taking tools for in-person meetings
3. **Steve:** Conduct discovery interviews this week
4. **Brayan:** Research USL Academy / USL2 market
5. **Brayan:** Generate platform name ideas
6. **Both:** Weekly check-in on data collection
7. **Target:** Data collection complete by next week

# Coach Steve Meeting — Follow-Up Questions
*April 13, 2026 — 2pm meeting with Brayan*

---

## Must-Answer Today

1. **How many clubs do you have direct relationships with right now?** We need a real number. 5? 20? 50? That's our launch roster.
2. **Would those clubs sign up before we build?** Can Coach Steve get 5-10 clubs to commit as early adopters? A marketplace with no supply is dead on arrival.
3. **What does a player profile need to have for a club coach to actually care?** Not what we think looks good. What does a coach actually look at when evaluating a kid?
4. **How do clubs find players today?** Word of mouth? Showcases? ID clinics? We need to know exactly what we're replacing.
5. **What age range are we targeting first?** U10 is different from U17. The needs, the parents, the stakes are all different. Pick one to start.

## Technical Scoping

6. **Where do highlight videos live right now?** YouTube? Hudl? Instagram? Do we host our own or embed from existing platforms? (Changes cost dramatically.)
7. **Is there a tryout/recruitment calendar he can share?** Knowing when demand spikes tells us what to build first.

## Business

8. **What would a club pay for this?** Has he talked to any club directors about pricing? Even a gut number helps.
9. **Who's the buyer at a club?** The head coach? Director of coaching? Club president? Need to know who we're building for on the club side.
10. **Does Coach Steve want to be the face of it?** Marketing, outreach, relationships. Is he doing sales calls to clubs, or is he an advisor?

## The Big One

11. **What does 50/50 actually mean day to day?** Brayan builds. What does Coach Steve do weekly? Signing up clubs? Creating content? Running demos? Define the split now before there's money involved.

title: One Ball One Dream: Call Plan - April 13 at 2pm
content:
# One Ball One Dream - Call Plan

*April 13, 2026 - 2:00 PM with Coach Steve*

---

## Questions to Ask Coach Steve

### Must-Answer Today
1. How many clubs do you have direct relationships with right now? We need a real number. 5? 20? 50? That's our launch roster.
2. Would those clubs sign up before we build? Can you get 5-10 clubs to commit as early adopters? A marketplace with no supply is dead on arrival.
3. What does a player profile need to have for a club coach to actually care? Not what we think looks good. What does a coach actually look at when evaluating a kid?
4. How do clubs find players today? Word of mouth? Showcases? ID clinics? We need to know exactly what we're replacing.
5. What age range are we targeting first? U10 is different from U17. The needs, the parents, the stakes are all different. Pick one to start.

### Technical Scoping
6. Where do highlight videos live right now? YouTube? Hudl? Instagram? Do we host our own or embed from existing platforms? (Changes cost dramatically.)
7. Is there a tryout/recruitment calendar you can share? Knowing when demand spikes tells us what to build first.

### Business
8. What would a club pay for this? Have you talked to any club directors about pricing? Even a gut number helps.
9. Who's the buyer at a club? The head coach? Director of coaching? Club president? We need to know who we're building for on the club side.
10. Do you want to be the face of it? Marketing, outreach, relationships. Are you doing sales calls to clubs, or are you an advisor?

### The Big One
11. What does 50/50 actually mean day to day? Brayan builds. What do you do weekly? Signing up clubs? Creating content? Running demos? We need to define the split now before there's money involved.

---

## The Point of This Call

One question drives everything: **Is Steve a co-founder or a dreamer?**

We're not formalizing a partnership today. We're figuring out if Steve has real relationships, can describe his own job, and is willing to do the work. If yes, we move to discovery. If he's vague on everything, we pump the brakes.

---

## Call Structure (30 min)

### Block 1: Kill the Fear (5 min)

Steve has two concerns. Address them fast and move on.

**"Someone will steal my idea"**
- "Coach, nobody you're talking to can build this. They're not developers. They're parents and coaches. Your relationships are what protects this - not secrecy. And you don't have to pitch anything. You're just asking questions you already ask as a coach."

**"My club will be repulsed"**
- "The platform doesn't just let players leave. It lets clubs FIND players. A club that needs a U14 forward mid-season can recruit through it. Good clubs benefit from this. And you don't need to tell your club anything right now. Discovery is quiet. Building is quiet. By launch, you can say 'I built something that helps the whole ecosystem.' That's leadership, not betrayal."

---

### Block 2: How Real Is This? (10 min)

Get concrete numbers. No vague answers. Push him if he's fuzzy.

**Question 1: "How many club directors could you text right now - not 'know of' - actually text and they'd respond?"**
- We need a real number. 5? 20? 50? This is the launch roster.
- If he says "a lot" or "I know people," push: "Give me names. Who are the first five?"

**Question 2: "You told me families are just Googling for clubs. Is that true on the club side too? When your club needs a player mid-season, how does that actually work?"**
- We already know the answer from the first call (Google, word of mouth, random phone calls), but let him go deeper. We want specifics.
- Follow up: "Is that how it works at every club you know, or is anyone doing it better?"

**Question 3: "What age group should we start with?"**
- If he can't pick one, he hasn't thought deeply enough. Push him.
- U10 parents care about fun and convenience. U17 parents care about college exposure. These are different products.
- We need ONE to start. His pick tells us a lot about his thinking.

**Question 4: "What does a coach actually look at when evaluating a kid? If a player profile showed up in front of you, what needs to be on it for you to care?"**
- Not what we think looks good. What does a real coach actually need to see?
- Position, age, location obviously. But what about video? Stats? Physical measurables? Tournament results?
- This shapes the entire MVP.

**Question 5: "Who's the buyer at a club? If we're selling this to clubs, who do you call - the head coach? Director of coaching? Club president?"**
- This tells us who the club-side user is. Critical for the sales motion later.

---

### Block 3: Define Steve's Job (10 min)

This is where you find out if he's a co-founder or a passenger.

**"Coach, if we move forward, what are you doing every week between now and January?"**
- Let him answer first. Don't feed him the answer.
- You want to hear him describe his own work: signing up clubs, talking to parents, being at showcases, doing demos.
- If he can't articulate it, that's a red flag.

**"Are you comfortable being the face of this? Doing demos, being on social media, running the outreach?"**
- Or does he want to be behind the scenes? Either is fine, but you need to know now.
- If he doesn't want to be the face AND doesn't want to do outreach, what exactly is his 50%?

**"What does 50/50 mean to you day to day?"**
- Brayan builds the platform, handles all tech, handles business operations.
- Steve does what? Be specific. Weekly commitments.
- Ideal answer: "I sign up clubs. I'm at every showcase recruiting users. I'm the one coaches call. I'm building the directory. I'm the face."
- Red flag answer: "I had the idea and I'll advise."

---

### Block 4: Next Steps + Homework (5 min)

**If he showed up real in blocks 2 and 3:**

"Here's what happens next. Before we write anything down on paper, we need to validate that people actually want this. You have 6 weeks of homework."

Steve's discovery assignment:
- **10 parents** - "When you were looking for a club for your kid, what was the hardest part? What do you wish existed?"
- **10 coaches/club directors** - "When you need to fill a roster spot mid-season, how do you find players? What's broken?"
- **5 players (older teens)** - "If you could put yourself out there to be seen by any club, what would that look like?"
- No pitching. Just listening. Write down the answers. Text or email Brayan notes after each conversation.

Tell him what you'll be doing in parallel:
- Competitive analysis (map existing platforms, find the gaps)
- Wireframing the MVP based on his discovery feedback
- Setting up the business entity and domain
- Drafting a partnership agreement for after discovery

Lock in the rhythm:
- Weekly 30-minute check-in call (pick a day/time)
- He sends discovery notes as they happen
- After 6 weeks (end of May), you reconvene with all the data and decide: build or don't build

**If he was vague on everything:**

"Coach, I love the idea. But I need to see the demand before I commit 9 months of building. Let's start small - talk to 10 people over the next 3 weeks and send me what you hear. If the data supports it, we go all in."

This protects your time without killing the relationship.

---

## What You Walk Away With

By the end of this call, you should know:

1. **A real number** of clubs Steve can activate (not "a lot" - names)
2. **How clubs find players today** (deeper than the first call)
3. **What age group we're targeting first**
4. **What a coach actually wants to see on a player profile**
5. **What Steve sees as his weekly job**
6. **Whether Steve is willing to do 25 discovery conversations**
7. **A weekly check-in day/time locked in**

If you have all seven, you're in great shape. The formal partnership agreement comes after discovery validates the market - late May or early June.

---

## What You Do NOT Do Today

- Don't walk through all 5 phases of the roadmap in detail. That's overwhelming. Just tell him "I have the full plan mapped out, we can walk through it once discovery confirms the market."
- Don't negotiate the 50/50 split. Too early. Just understand what he thinks his contribution is.
- Don't talk about technical implementation, tech stack, or features beyond the profile question. He doesn't need to know how it's built.
- Don't promise a timeline for the build. Just say "once discovery is done, I'll have wireframes ready and we start building."

---

## Cheat Sheet (Keep This Open During the Call)

| What you need | What to ask | Red flag | Green flag |
|---|---|---|---|
| Club network size | "How many directors could you text right now?" | "A lot" / no names | Specific names and numbers |
| Current process | "How does your club find a player mid-season?" | "I'm not sure" | Detailed breakdown of the mess |
| Age focus | "What age group first?" | Can't pick one | Picks one with reasoning |
| Profile needs | "What does a coach look at?" | "Everything" | Specific: "Video, position, tournament results" |
| His weekly job | "What are you doing every week?" | "I'll advise" | "I'll sign up clubs, do demos, be at showcases" |
| Discovery willingness | "25 conversations in 6 weeks?" | Hesitates / excuses | "I can do that this month" |
| Face of the brand | "Are you comfortable being the face?" | Won't commit either way | Clear yes or clear no with alternative |

title: Coach Steve: One Ball One Dream - Youth Soccer Platform Partnership
content:
# Coach Steve - One Ball One Dream Partnership

## Who is Coach Steve?

- Brayan's former youth soccer coach from Danbury, CT
- Coached Brayan through state championships and regionals
- Currently coaches volleyball and youth soccer
- Deep relationships across the Connecticut youth soccer community
- Long-standing personal relationship with Brayan built on trust

## The Platform Concept: One Ball One Dream

Coach Steve's brainchild. A youth soccer marketplace/portal that connects players with clubs at all levels.

### The Problem He Sees
- Clubs failing kids with false promises, selling a bag of goods for money
- No centralized platform for players to showcase themselves to clubs
- When families relocate, they rely on Google to find clubs - no dedicated tool
- Player movement happens year-round, not just at tryouts (mid-season transfers, ID clinics, new arrivals)
- Kids who get cut from MLS clubs need to find ECNL/travel clubs quickly, and vice versa

### Platform Vision
- **Player profiles:** Highlights, stats, contact info - all in one place
- **Club discovery:** AI-powered matching based on location, skill level, age, preferences (e.g., "I'm in 8th grade, live in Danbury, want to play ECNL within 20 miles")
- **All levels:** MLS, ECNL, travel, recreational
- **Year-round:** Not bounded by tryout seasons. Open platform for mid-season transfers, ID clinics, relocations
- **All-in-one:** LinkedIn for soccer players - video hosting, communication tools, automated profile submission to scouting events
- **Club side:** Clubs can search for specific player types (e.g., "we need a forward on our U14 team")

### Revenue Model
- **Premium profiles:** Paid boosting for visibility (like Instagram promotion). Tiered pricing for basic vs enhanced.
- **Advertising:** Soccer sponsors advertising to large player base
- **Data value:** Clubs pay for access to player data/search

### Brand
- **Name:** "One Ball One Dream"
- **Concept:** Each player has a ball at their feet and gets to decide their own future

## Partnership Structure

- 50/50 partnership - Coach Steve (soccer industry expertise, connections, credibility) + Brayan (builds entire technical platform)
- **Timeline:** Development throughout 2026, launch January 2027 aligned with club recruitment cycles

## Data Privacy
- Parent permission systems required for minors
- Leveraging data privacy compliance knowledge from ed-tech work with schools

## Market Research Notes

### Why This Market Works
- Youth soccer is massive - MLS, ECNL, travel, and rec clubs across the country
- No existing centralized platform for player-club matching
- Families relocate constantly and have no tool to find clubs in new areas
- ID clinics happen year-round but discovery is fragmented
- Clubs actively need to find players mid-season (not just at tryouts)
- The "LinkedIn for soccer" model has been validated in other sports verticals

### Comparable Model
- Similar marketplace structure to the Furfect Match project (pet adoption marketplace) but different vertical
- Same technical foundation: AI-powered matching, profiles, discovery

### Competitive Advantage
- Coach Steve brings insider credibility and relationships across CT youth soccer
- AI-powered matching is a differentiator vs basic directories
- All-in-one approach (video, communication, profiles, scouting) vs fragmented tools

content:
# Rostam Mahabadi Meeting Notes — April 10, 2026

## What Ryan Shared with Rostam

### NGL Education Programs
- 5 programs running across Connecticut: Danbury, Ridgefield, Boys and Girls Clubs
- Hands-on workshops where kids build websites, learn animation, discover their interests
- Used Lovable to teach kids to build websites. One girl built a jump rope products website, ran outside to show her mom. Kids were fully engaged.
- Just went to Woodstock, CT and taught 50 teachers how to use Jarvis for education
- One teacher took the app and implemented it in her third grade classroom for teaching money concepts
- Started from personal experience: struggled with traditional learning, always wanted to discover and learn on his own. Built programs so kids can learn what they want to learn about.

### NGL Business Pivot
- Building a Board of Trustees
- New approach: going into businesses, talking to owners, figuring out their pain points, then building systems around that
- Not pre-building and selling. Talking first to see if they can even help before making anything.
- About 6-7 days into this new approach
- **12 active leads right now**, 3 are extremely interested (communicating almost every day)
- Building demo systems for the hot leads, demo meetings within next few days
- Example: building an email pipeline that scans businesses in an area, then sends emails all at once (the Furfetch pipeline)
- Another call at 3:30 to try to close
- Has Jarvis system tracking leads and pipeline

### Work Ethic and Tracking
- Built a time tracker app to track hours
- 40 hours logged Monday through Thursday
- Aiming for a 70-hour week

### Pricing Challenge
- Going into businesses and building different software solutions
- Not sure how to price them. Lots of variables. Acknowledged this as an honest gap.

## What Ryan Asked Rostam

- **Close rate:** How did you get to 90-95%? How did those numbers get so high?

title: Proposal: AI Literacy Program at Mount Pleasant Library
content:
# Proposal: AI Literacy Program
## Mount Pleasant Public Library, Pleasantville, NY

*Prepared for Janet Green, Head of Youth Services*
*April 9, 2026*

---

## Program Overview

A 3-week AI literacy workshop series for students in grades 4-8. One session per week (Mondays), starting Fall 2026. Each session is a standalone experience so students can attend one, two, or all three.

---

## Format

- **Duration:** 3 sessions, once per week (Mondays)
- **Session length:** 90 minutes each
- **Audience:** Grades 4-8
- **Group size:** 10-20 students recommended
- **Season:** Fall 2026 (exact dates TBD with Janet)

---

## What Students Learn

Each session is hands-on. Students don't sit and listen. They build, question, and think critically about AI.

**Three core skills:**
1. **Prompt Engineering:** How to communicate clearly with AI through precise, thoughtful prompts
2. **Output Verification:** How to evaluate and verify what AI produces before trusting it
3. **Ethical Reasoning:** Understanding the responsibilities of using AI

**The "Different Means, Same End" approach:**
Whether a student loves art, music, coding, or entrepreneurship, they use their own interest as the path to AI literacy. The artsy kid designs with AI. The coder builds with AI. The quiet kid finds their angle. Everyone ends up with the same critical thinking skills.

---

## Session Structure (90 minutes)

| Block | Time | What Happens |
|-------|------|-------------|
| The Hook | 15 min | Live AI demos: impressive and flawed. Kids see what AI can and can't do |
| Socratic Discussion | 20 min | Hard questions: "Who made this? Is it biased? Why did it give that answer?" |
| The Build | 45 min | Students use AI tools to solve a creative challenge tied to their interests |
| The Debrief | 10 min | Students present work and reflect on where AI helped vs. got in the way |

---

## What We've Seen in Our Programs

Results from pilots across 3 CT libraries (Danbury, Ridgefield, Easton), grades 2-8:

- **Fear to Ownership:** Students arrived thinking AI was magic. By session 3-4, they were revising prompts and disagreeing with outputs
- **Students asked to stay:** Kids were disappointed when sessions ended
- **Thinking became visible:** Before-and-after mind maps showed measurable growth in clarity and confidence
- **Partner, not employee:** When students treated AI as a partner instead of a shortcut, they shifted from consuming answers to making decisions

---

## What the Library Provides

- A room with tables and chairs
- Wi-Fi access
- That's it. We handle everything else.

## What NGL Provides

- Full curriculum (designed, tested, refined across multiple programs)
- All instruction and facilitation
- Age-appropriate AI tools and interfaces (walled-garden, safe for youth)
- Materials and handouts
- Post-program summary for the library

---

## Investment

**$150-175 per session** (90 minutes)

3 sessions total: **$450-525 for the full program**

This is in line with what Mount Pleasant typically pays for outside programming.

---

## Our Track Record

- **3 library programs** completed (Danbury, Ridgefield, Easton)
- **50+ students** trained across grades K-8
- **50+ educators** trained at Woodstock Public Schools PD Day
- **Wooster School:** Student and faculty sessions
- **Founded at Babson College**

---

## Next Steps

1. Janet confirms Fall dates and preferred Mondays
2. We lock in the 3-week schedule
3. NGL builds a custom session plan for Mount Pleasant's audience
4. Library promotes the program to families
5. We show up and deliver

---

*Ryan Vincent*
*Co-Founder, Next Generation Learners*
*ryanvincent@nextgenerationlearners.com*

title: Eiker Jacome — Ad Tracking Tool (Hyros Alternative)
content:
# Eiker Jacome — Ad Tracking Tool Project
*April 8, 2026 — Ryan Vincent*
*Updated: April 13, 2026 — Discovery complete*

---

## Source
Instagram DM from Eiker Jacome.

## The Ask
Build a custom ad tracking / attribution platform that replaces Hyros. Hyros is the current industry standard for ad tracking but has two major problems: inaccurate data and expensive pricing ($300-1000+/mo).

Eiker wants a tool that:
1. **More accurate attribution** — track the full journey from ad click to sale without the data gaps Hyros has
2. **More granular data** — not just "this ad made $X" but deeper breakdowns (which ad, which audience, which funnel step)
3. **Backend client success stats** — not just front-end metrics (clicks, leads) but what happens after the sale: client retention, results, lifetime value
4. **Cheaper than Hyros**

## Discovery Answers (April 10, 2026)
1. **Ad platforms:** Meta only
2. **Sales funnel:** Book-a-call funnel
3. **Payment/CRM stack:** Fanbasis (payment), GoHighLevel (CRM)
4. **What's broken with Hyros:** Not fully broken. Has miscalculations in attribution data plus missing tracking for client sales
5. **Client success stats:** Means tracking what his sales team has done individually when signing new clients. Per-rep performance visibility.
6. **Use case:** Personal/internal use only

## Confirmed Scope
- **Internal tool** — weeks to build, not months
- Pipeline: Meta Ads API → Fanbasis → GHL → custom dashboard
- Key deliverables: accurate attribution, client sales tracking, individual sales rep performance
- No pixel infrastructure needed (Meta-only + call funnel = UTMs + Meta CAPI)
- No multi-tenant, onboarding, or billing needed

Discovery complete — ready to scope and build.

title: Strategic Blocker Plan: Time Management & Environment
content:
# Strategic Blocker Plan: Time Management & Environment

*Created: 2026-04-04*
*Owner: Ryan*
*Horizon: 90 days (by 2026-07-04)*

---

## The Real Problem

You're not unproductive because you're lazy. You're unproductive because your environment doesn't protect your time, and the people around you aren't on the same clock. Every day is a negotiation between your future and your present social life — and without a system, the present wins more than it should.

There are two levers to fix this: **structure your time** and **upgrade your environment**.

---

## Lever 1: Time Structure

### The Core Principle
You can't manage what you don't track. Right now, time is invisible. The first move is making it visible.

### Actions

**Week 1 — Audit**
- For 7 days, log every hour. What did you actually do? Use your phone's notes app, a simple spreadsheet, or any tool. Don't judge it — just capture it.
- At the end of 7 days, look at where time leaked. You'll see the pattern.

**Week 2 — Design your ideal week**
- Block your non-negotiable work hours first. Minimum 4 focused hours per day on business-building (outreach, product, strategy). Treat these like appointments you cannot cancel.
- Assign specific tasks to specific blocks. "Work on business" is not a block. "Call 5 schools" or "build feature X" is a block.
- Schedule fun. Skimboarding and surfing go on the calendar too — fixed times, not open-ended. This removes the guilt and removes the infinite availability.

**Daily anchor habit**
- Every morning: write 3 things you will accomplish today before the day is done.
- Every night: score yourself. Did you do them? Why or why not?
- This takes 5 minutes and builds the discipline feedback loop.

**Tool recommendation**
- Restart Monday.com with your business partner, but keep it simple: 3 columns — To Do, In Progress, Done. One board for business development (school outreach), one for product.
- Alternatively, use a simple daily note system if Monday feels heavy. The tool matters less than the habit.

---

## Lever 2: Environment Upgrade

### The Core Principle
You become the average of who you spend time with. Right now your average doesn't include enough builders. That's not a character flaw in your friends — it's just math. Fix the inputs.

### Actions

**Find your people (ongoing)**
- Identify 2-3 local founders or builders you respect or want to be around. Even one person in your city who is building something changes your baseline.
- Go to founder meetups, startup events, and entrepreneurship groups in your area. You were just at a conference — that's the right instinct. Keep going to those.
- LinkedIn outreach to founders in edtech or education technology who are 1-2 years ahead of you. Ask for a 20-minute call. Most will say yes.

**Create accountability**
- Find one accountability partner — a person who is also building — and do weekly check-ins. 15 minutes every Monday. What did you commit to last week? Did you do it? What are you committing to this week?
- This makes your goals real and witnessed. It changes the social pressure from "come hang out" to "did you hit your number?"

**Protect your time from friends without burning relationships**
- You don't have to disappear. You have to be unavailable during work blocks and fully present during scheduled downtime.
- Practice saying: "I'm heads down until 4pm, let's link after." Simple. No guilt required.
- Friends who respect you will respect the boundary. The ones who don't — that's information.

**Longer-term: move toward the environment you want**
- If your 90-day goal is to land 2-3 clients and talk to 100+ schools, that performance will start opening doors to different rooms — accelerators, founder communities, investor networks. Stay focused on the 90-day numbers and the environment will start to upgrade itself.

---

## 90-Day Milestones

| Week | Focus | Goal |
|------|-------|------|
| 1 | Audit | Know exactly where your time is going |
| 2 | Design | Ideal week built, work blocks protected |
| 3-4 | Outreach begins | First 20 school conversations |
| 5-8 | Momentum | 50+ school conversations, 1 serious prospect |
| 9-12 | Close | 100+ conversations, 2-3 clients signed |

**Non-business goal running in parallel:** Daily physical routine locked in by end of week 2. Body and focus are infrastructure — they're not separate from the business goal.

---

## The One Thing

If you only do one thing from this plan, do this:

**Design your ideal week this weekend and live it for 14 days straight.**

Everything else — the environment, the clients, the body — follows from having protected time to actually work. Time is the first domino.

---

*Review this plan in 30 days. What's working? What isn't? Update accordingly.*
